risks: Pod Identity (aad-pod-identity) is deprecated as of 10/2022; plan references podIdentity which will not receive updates or support
Open Service Mesh (OSM) is retired; relying on it introduces long-term maintenance risk
AKS subnet 10.0.0.0/24 provides only 251 usable IPs; with Azure CNI (default 30 pods/node), 3 nodes consume ~93 IPs leaving limited headroom for scaling or upgrades
Application Gateway is referenced in ingress notes but is not defined as a resource, so AGIC cannot be configured without manual addition
No availability zone configuration for AKS node pools or other resources, reducing resilience to datacenter-level failures
AKS API server is publicly accessible by default; no private cluster or authorized IP range configuration is specified
No explicit managed identity or role assignment definitions (e.g., acrpull for kubelet identity to ACR)
correctionsRecommended: Replace podIdentity with Microsoft Entra Workload Identity (workload-identity-overview) for secure pod-to-Azure-resource access
Replace Open Service Mesh with the Istio-based service mesh add-on for AKS, which is the supported alternative
Add Application Gateway as an explicit resource with a dedicated subnet (e.g., 10.0.1.0/24) if AGIC is intended
Expand AKS subnet from /24 to /22 or larger to provide scaling headroom for Azure CNI networking
Add availability zone configuration (zones: [1,2,3]) to AKS node pools for zone-redundant resilience
Configure private AKS cluster or add API server authorized IP ranges to limit public exposure
Define managed identity resources and AcrPull role assignment from AKS kubelet identity to ACR
Add service CIDR and DNS service IP explicitly to avoid potential overlap with future VNet expansions
hardDependencies: Application Gateway requires a dedicated subnet not shared with AKS; if AGIC ingress is used, a separate subnet must be provisioned before deployment
